mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates robust economic activity and consumer demand. This typically supports corporate earnings and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth typically correlates with higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive (3-month average > 0.1%) but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a certain threshold, it signals potential future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. This tends to be a headwind for equity markets as discount rates increase and corporate margins are squeezed, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation often precedes central bank rate hikes.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds the trigger for 3 consecutive months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and declining unemployment indicates robust consumer health and spending power. This drives corporate revenues and supports equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending and employment are key drivers of economic growth.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and UNRATE conditions are met, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS), equities are more attractive relative to bonds, leading to higher valuations. This effect is amplified when rates are near historical lows, supporting positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Lower discount rates generally boost equity valuations.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations during that period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic contraction. This typically leads to lower corporate earnings and negative equity market performance, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls below the trigger for 2 consecutive months, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence. This has positive spillover effects across the economy and supports equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength is often a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent positive growth (3-month average > 0.5%) for 6 months, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations during that period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and rising inflation pressures the central bank to tighten monetary policy. This leads to higher discount rates and a negative outlook for equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% AND CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Tight labor markets and inflation often lead to hawkish monetary policy.
failure_condition: If all three conditions are met, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction (negative GDP growth), corporate earnings decline significantly, leading to widespread selling pressure on equities and negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are typically associated with equity market downturns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows negative growth for two consecutive quarters, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of observations during that period.
